about.title: About Me
about.bio: I am a backend engineer focused on high-performance and resilient systems. My core stack is Rust, Go, and Java, and I enjoy designing service boundaries, optimizing latency, and shipping production-ready APIs. [Add your detailed bio here].
about.interestsList: Distributed Systems, API Design, Performance Tuning, Cloud Infrastructure [Edit here]

items.title: [Project 1 Name]
items.description: A high-concurrency user and auth service for B2B platforms, with request tracing and rate limiting. Built with Go and Redis. [Add your project description here].

items.title: [Project 2 Name]
items.description: An async job processing and scheduling system designed for reliability and throughput. Built with Rust and message queues. [Add your project description here].

items.title: [Project 3 Name]
items.description: A Java microservice handling order workflows with idempotency, retries, and transaction safety. [Add your project description here].

items.title: [Project 4 Name]
items.description: A backend platform toolkit that standardizes deployment, monitoring, and service health checks across teams. [Add your project description here].

jobs.description: Designed and delivered core microservices in Rust and Go, improved API latency, and introduced observability standards for production systems.
Built Java and Go services for internal platforms, owned API contracts, and delivered stable integrations with databases and message queues.
Maintained backend modules, wrote integration tests, and supported release pipelines for service deployments.

items.description: Specialized in Human-Computer Interaction and Software Engineering. Graduated with Honors.
Foundational studies in algorithms, data structures, and system design.
